prose.en: When selecting server applications, it is important that an organisation preferences vendors that have demonstrated a commitment to Secure by Design and Secure by Default principles and practices. This includes secure programming practices and either memory-safe programming languages (such as C#, Go, Java, Ruby, Rust and Swift) or less preferably memory-safe programming practices. This will assist in not only reducing the potential number of vulnerabilities in server applications, but also increasing the likelihood that timely patches, updates or vendor mitigations will be released to remediate any vulnerabilities that are found.
